oligos.name: pMENTs
oligos.role: Tn5 mosaic-end bottom (non-transferred) strand, 3' ddC blocked
oligos.notes: Written 5'->3' as /5Phos/CTGTCTCTTATACACATCTddC. Common phosphorylated ME complement annealed to every transferred adaptor (AdaptorA or barcoded DNA_#_RE) to form the Tn5 transposome; terminal dideoxy-C blocks extension.

oligos.name: AdaptorA (Tn5-P5 transferred strand)
oligos.role: Nextera/P5 read-1 tagmentation adaptor added in second tagging step
oligos.notes: TCGTCGGCAGCGTC (Nextera Read1) + AGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG (19 nt mosaic end). Loaded onto Tn5 (Tn5-P5) and used to tagment the pre-amplified insert, adding the P5/Read1 handle.

oligos.name: DNA_#01-08_RE (barcoded Tn5 adaptor, transferred strand)
oligos.role: Round-1 (BC#1) cell barcode + NotI site + mosaic end for open-chromatin tagmentation
oligos.notes: 5'-phosphorylated. Example DNA_#01_RE = /5Phos/AGGCCAGAGCATTCGAGATCGCGGCCGCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG (barcode AGATC). Eight barcodes A1-A8. Annealed to pMENTs and loaded onto Tn5. The NotI (GCGGCCGC) site allows the RNA-library digestion to destroy DNA molecules.

oligos.name: RNA_#01-08_RE (barcoded RT primer)
oligos.role: Round-1 (BC#1) cell barcode + SbfI site + anchored poly(dT) RT primer
oligos.notes: 5'-phosphorylated. Example RNA_#01_RE = /5Phos/AGGCCAGAGCATTCGTCATCCCTGCAGGTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTTVN (barcode TCATC). Same 8 wells/order as the DNA Tn5 adaptors so DNA and cDNA share the same first-round barcode. SbfI (CCTGCAGG) lets the DNA-library digestion destroy RNA molecules. V=A/C/G, N=any.

oligos.name: Linker-R02
oligos.role: Splint bridging round-1 product 5' end to R02 barcode for ligation
oligos.notes: Reverse complement = ATCCACGTGCTTGAGAGGCCAGAGCATTCG (= Blocker-R02). CGAATGCTCTGGCCT is complementary to the round-1 linker AGGCCAGAGCATTCG; CTCAAGCACGTGGAT is complementary to the R02 3' constant ATCCACGTGCTTGAG.

oligos.name: Linker-R03
oligos.role: Splint bridging round-2 product 5' end to R03 barcode for ligation
oligos.notes: GGTCTGAGTTCGCAC is complementary to R02 5' constant GTGCGAACTCAGACC; ACGCTCCTCGAATCC is complementary to R03 3' constant GATTCGAGGAGCGT.

oligos.name: Linker-R04
oligos.role: Splint bridging round-3 product 5' end to R04 barcode for ligation
oligos.notes: AGTCGTACGCCGATG is complementary to R03 5' constant CATCGGCGTACGACT; CGAAACATCGGCCAC is complementary to R04 3' constant GTGGCCGATGTTTCG.

oligos.name: Blocker-R02
oligos.role: Blocking oligo added after round-2 ligation to occupy the round-1 handle/splint
oligos.notes: Reverse complement of Linker-R02; blocks unreacted linker after ligation round 1.

oligos.name: Blocker-R03
oligos.role: Blocking oligo added after round-3 ligation
oligos.notes: Reverse complement of Linker-R03; blocks unreacted linker after ligation round 2.

oligos.name: Terminator (R04)
oligos.role: Terminating/blocking oligo added after the final (round-4) ligation
oligos.notes: Reverse complement of Linker-R04; quenches the final ligation. GTGGCCGATGTTTCG = R04 3' constant, CATCGGCGTACGACT = R03 5' constant.

oligos.name: R02_#01-96 (round-2 barcode oligo)
oligos.role: Round-2 (BC#2) cell barcode ligated by Linker-R02 splint

oligos.name: R03_#01-96 (round-3 barcode oligo)
oligos.role: Round-3 (BC#3) cell barcode ligated by Linker-R03 splint

oligos.name: R04_#01-96 (round-4 barcode oligo, terminal)
oligos.role: Round-4 (BC#4) cell barcode + UMI + Read2/PA-F handle (final ligation)
oligos.notes: NOT 5'-phosphorylated (terminal round). Example R04_#01 = CAGACGTGTGCTCTTCCGATCTNNNNNNNNNNAAACCGGGTGGCCGATGTTTCG (10 nt UMI, barcode AAACCGG). Its 5' CAGACGTGTGCTCTTCCGATCT becomes the Read2 primer binding site.

oligos.name: Anchor
oligos.role: Poly(dG) anchor oligo priming the poly(dC)-tailed 3' end during linear amplification (adds PA-R sequence)
oligos.notes: AAGCAGTGGTATCAACGCAGAGT (ISPCR/PA-R handle) + GAATG spacer + poly(dG9)H. Anneals to the TdT poly(dC) tail and is extended by KAPA HiFi, appending the PA-R handle to the insert-distal end.

oligos.name: PA-F
oligos.notes: Matches the 5' handle of the R04 barcode oligo; also the Read2 primer sequence and the 3' end of the P7 indexing primer.

oligos.name: PA-R
oligos.notes: Matches the ISPCR handle appended by the Anchor oligo.

oligos.name: N5XX (i5 indexing primer)
oligos.role: Final PCR P5 + i5 index primer (anneals to AdaptorA/Nextera Read1)

oligos.name: P7XX (i7 indexing primer)
oligos.role: Final PCR P7 + i7 index primer (anneals to PA-F/Read2 handle)

final_library.source_label: Paired-seq DNA (ATAC) sublibrary; RNA sublibrary differs only at the insert junction (see notes)
final_library.annotated_library_sequence: P5 | i5(8) | Nextera-R1+ME | INSERT | ME'(NotI) | BC1(5) | linkerR1'+R02arm | BC2(7) | R02handle'+R03arm | BC3(8) | R03handle'+R04arm | BC4(7) | UMI(10) | Read2(PA-F)' | P7-linker | i7(6) | P7
annotation_lines: TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG = Nextera Read1 handle + mosaic end (from Tn5-P5/AdaptorA); Read1 primer site
[CDNA] = genomic open-chromatin insert (DNA library) or cDNA insert (RNA library)
CTGTCTCTTATACACATCT = reverse complement of the BC#1 mosaic end (Tn5 barcoded adaptor)
GCGGCCGC = NotI site (DNA library; replaced by SbfI CCTGCAGG + poly(dA) in the RNA library)
CGAATGCTCTGGCCTCTCAAGCACGTGGAT = round-1 linker + R02 splint arm (rev-comp)
GGTCTGAGTTCGCACACGCTCCTCGAATC = R02 handle + R03 splint arm (rev-comp)
AGTCGTACGCCGATGCGAAACATCGGCCAC = R03 handle + R04 splint arm (rev-comp)
AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTG = reverse complement of PA-F = Read2 primer site

evidence.locator: Paired-seq DNA (ATAC) sublibrary; RNA sublibrary differs only at the insert junction (see notes)

library_generation.title: Assemble barcoded Tn5 transposomes
library_generation.summary: Anneal 8 barcoded DNA_#_RE adaptors to the common pMENTs strand and load onto Tn5.
library_generation.note: Each transposome carries a first-round (BC#1) barcode, a NotI site, and the mosaic end; also a separate Tn5-P5 (AdaptorA) transposome is made for the later second-tagging step.

library_generation.title: In situ tagmentation (round-1 DNA barcode)
library_generation.summary: Barcoded Tn5 tagments accessible chromatin in 8 wells, attaching BC#1 + ME to open-chromatin fragments.
library_generation.note: Carried out on intact nuclei; 8 different Tn5 barcodes label 8 wells.

library_generation.title: Reverse transcription (round-1 RNA barcode)
library_generation.summary: Barcoded anchored poly(dT) primers (RNA_#_RE, same 8 wells) reverse-transcribe mRNA into barcoded cDNA.
library_generation.note: cDNA receives the same well/first-round barcode as the DNA, plus an SbfI site and poly(dT).

library_generation.title: Ligation round 2 (BC#2)
library_generation.summary: Splint (Linker-R02) ligates the R02 barcode onto the 5' end of every DNA and cDNA molecule.
library_generation.note: Nuclei pooled and split into a 96-well R02 plate; Blocker-R02 quenches unreacted linker.

library_generation.title: Ligation round 3 (BC#3)
library_generation.summary: Splint (Linker-R03) ligates the R03 barcode onto the new 5' end.
library_generation.note: Pool/split into a 96-well R03 plate; Blocker-R03 quenches unreacted linker.

library_generation.title: Ligation round 4 (BC#4 + UMI + Read2 handle)
library_generation.summary: Splint (Linker-R04) ligates the terminal R04 barcode, adding BC#4, a 10 nt UMI and the PA-F/Read2 handle.
library_generation.note: R04 lacks a 5' phosphate (terminal); Terminator oligo + EDTA quench the reaction. After 3 ligation rounds >10^7 barcode combinations exist.

library_generation.title: Split to sublibraries, lyse & purify
library_generation.summary: Barcoded nuclei are counted, split into ~25k-nucleus sublibraries, proteinase-K lysed and SPRI-purified.
library_generation.note: Releases barcoded genomic-DNA and cDNA molecules for amplification.
library_generation.product: single-stranded barcoded DNA/cDNA molecules (both modalities intermixed)

library_generation.title: TdT poly(dC) tailing
library_generation.summary: Terminal transferase adds a poly(dC) tail to the free 3' end of each molecule.
library_generation.note: Adopted from the TELP single-stranded amplification method.

library_generation.title: Anchor-primed linear amplification
library_generation.summary: The poly(dG) Anchor oligo primes the poly(dC) tail and KAPA extension appends the PA-R handle.
library_generation.note: 15 cycles of linear amplification convert single strands into PA-F/PA-R-flanked amplicons.

library_generation.title: Pre-amplification PCR (PA-F + PA-R)
library_generation.summary: 10 cycles with PA-F and PA-R generate double-stranded, barcoded amplicons.
library_generation.note: SPRI double-size selection cleans the products.

library_generation.title: Split + restriction digestion (library dedicating)
library_generation.summary: Products are split; the DNA tube is cut with SbfI (destroys RNA/cDNA molecules) and the RNA tube with NotI (destroys DNA molecules).
library_generation.note: Because cDNA carries SbfI and genomic molecules carry NotI, each digestion removes the opposite modality, dedicating pure DNA and RNA libraries.
library_generation.product: DNA library: NotI-containing amplicons intact | RNA library: SbfI-containing amplicons intact

library_generation.title: Second adaptor tagging (Tn5-P5)
library_generation.summary: Tn5 loaded with AdaptorA tagments the insert end, adding the P5/Read1 (Nextera) handle.
library_generation.note: Provides the Read1 sequencing primer site on the insert side.

library_generation.title: Indexing PCR (N5XX + P7XX)
library_generation.summary: Final PCR adds P5+i5 (N5XX) and P7+i7 (P7XX), completing the sequenceable library.
library_generation.note: 10-15 cycles to ~10 nM; 0.85x SPRI cleanup. Sequenced on HiSeq 2500.

library_sequencing.primer: Nextera Read1 primer (TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG, from N5XX/Tn5-P5 AdaptorA)
library_sequencing.note: Reads the tagmented genomic open-chromatin fragment (DNA library) or the cDNA (RNA library). Primer 3' end abuts the insert and extends across it.
library_sequencing.diagram:
Read 1 primer
   5'-TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG----------->
5'-...TCGTCGGCAGCGTCAGATGTGTATAAGAGACAG[====INSERT 53 nt====]...-3'  (top, reported)
      |||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||||
3'-...AGCAGCCGTCGCAGTCTACACATATTCTCTGTC[====insert  compl====]...-5'  (bottom, template)

library_sequencing.primer: i7 index-read primer (anneals to the P7XX read2/P7 linker, ...GAACTCCAGTCAC-3')
library_sequencing.note: Reads the 6 nt i7 sample index added by P7XX; 7 cycles cover the 6 nt index. Primer anneals to the top strand just 5' of the index and copies the bottom strand.

Reads 10 nt UMI then the four ligation barcode rounds interleaved with linkers: BC#4 first base ~pos 10-13, BC#3 ~47-50, BC#2 ~84-87, BC#1 ~121-124. Primer anneals to the top-strand Read2 site (AGATCGGAAGAGCACACGTCTG) and extends leftward (3' end toward the UMI), so it reports the bottom-strand sequence.

title: Paired-seq
description: Paired-seq (parallel analysis of individual cells for RNA expression and DNA accessibility by sequencing) is an ultra high-throughput combinatorial-indexing assay that jointly profiles open chromatin (ATAC) and the transcriptome from the same single nucleus. Nuclei are Tn5-tagmented with barcoded mosaic-end adaptors and reverse-transcribed with barcoded poly(dT) primers in the same wells, then given three additional rounds of splint-ligation barcoding, generating >10^7 unique cell barcode combinations. Amplified molecules are split and digested with NotI or SbfI to "dedicate" DNA vs RNA libraries. Read 1 sequences the genomic/cDNA insert; Read 2 (130 cyc) reads a 10 nt UMI followed by the four cell-barcode rounds; Index 1 reads the i7 sample index.

original_publication.title: An ultra high-throughput method for single-cell joint analysis of open chromatin and transcriptome

authors.name: Chenxu Zhu

throughput.summary: Ultra high-throughput: five rounds of combinatorial indexing (8 x 96 x 96 x 96 x PCR) yield >10^7 barcode combinations, enabling joint DNA+RNA profiling of up to millions of nuclei per experiment.
throughput.cells: 1.51M (cell lines, 18.9% recovery), 2.51M (adult cortex, 25.1%) and 0.56M (fetal forebrain, 9.3%) barcoded nuclei recovered; sequenced sublibraries of ~10k-30k nuclei; barcode collision rate <3%.
throughput.rna: Median ~1,236-1,872 uniquely mapped RNA reads per nucleus (cell lines); ~1,166 (adult cortex).
throughput.dna: Median ~1,641-2,635 uniquely mapped DNA reads per nucleus (cell lines); ~1,762 (adult cortex); 188,460 CREs identified in cortex.

publication.statistical_model: Negative binomial (edgeR) for differential gene expression / CRE accessibility; Pearson correlation across pseudo-cells for gene-CRE linkage; positive Poisson regression (MAPS) for companion PLAC-seq contacts.

other.label: Library dedicating
other.value: SbfI digests RNA molecules from the DNA tube; NotI digests DNA molecules from the RNA tube

other.label: Amplification
other.value: TdT poly(dC) tailing + anchor-primed linear amplification (adapted from TELP)

data_processing.summary: Cell barcodes and their intervening linkers are parsed from Read 2 and decoded by building a Bowtie2 index of every possible barcode combination and mapping the concatenated barcode reads to it; the genomic/cDNA insert (Read 1 + UMI) is then aligned to the genome with STAR, deduplicated by mapping position + UMI, and split into a DNA (ATAC, cell-by-bin) and RNA (cell-by-gene) matrix that are integrated by SnapATAC (Jaccard similarity + Hadamard product) for joint clustering.
data_processing.statistical_model: Negative binomial (edgeR) for differential expression / CRE accessibility; Pearson correlation across 50-nucleus pseudo-cells for gene-CRE linkage
